No Poverty.reason: The course focuses on fundamental accounting principles and financial statement analysis, with no explicit or strong inferred connection to poverty eradication or related financial mechanisms.

Zero Hunger.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus that directly or indirectly relates to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture practices.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course content does not include any topics related to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare financing.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly contributes to quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with professional skills for employment (Target 4.4) in accounting.
evidence: provide a fundamental coverage of accounting concepts
奠定相關基礎專業知識(Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)

Gender Equality.reason: There is no explicit or strongly inferred evidence in the syllabus regarding gender equality, women's empowerment, or gender-sensitive accounting practices.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: The course content does not address issues related to clean water, sanitation, water management, or pollution reduction.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: There is no evidence in the syllabus concerning affordable and clean energy, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides essential accounting skills for analyzing financial statements, which is fundamental for promoting productive activities, decent job creation, and strengthening financial institutions (Targets 8.3, 8.10).
evidence: 奠定相關基礎專業知識

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on financing (long-term liabilities, equity) and investments (plant assets) provides foundational knowledge for enterprises to access financial services and build industrial capacity, aligning with Target 9.3.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The ability to interpret and analyze financial statements contributes to the transparency and effective regulation of financial markets, which can indirectly support the reduction of inequalities (Target 10.5).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course syllabus does not contain any content related to sustainable cities, urban planning, housing, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly aims to develop students' ability to 'identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' which is crucial for companies to adopt sustainable practices and integrate sustainability information into reporting (Target 12.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題(Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.)

Climate Action.reason: The course's focus on 'sustainability issues' (Core Competency B) can be very broadly inferred to include climate-related financial reporting or impact assessment, though it's not explicit.

Life Below Water.reason: There is no evidence in the course syllabus related to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable use of marine resources.

Life on Land.reason: The course includes 'Natural Resources' as a topic and emphasizes 'sustainability issues,' which can be inferred to include accounting for natural capital and integrating biodiversity values (Target 15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on financial statement preparation and ethical considerations directly supports the development of effective, accountable, and transparent institutions and the reduction of corruption (Targets 16.5, 16.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別道德、社會責任與永續議題

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's emphasis on financial statement preparation and analysis provides skills essential for domestic resource mobilization (Target 17.1) and for generating reliable data to measure SDG progress (Target 17.18, 17.19).
